title: Your First Brief
context: Welcome to Brief Quest! A client has sent their first brief for a new website. Let's sort through what's really needed, what's nice to have, and what's just noise.

requirements.text: The website must load in under 3 seconds
requirements.explanation: Performance is a hard requirement — slow sites lose visitors and affect SEO.

requirements.text: It would be nice to have an animated hero banner
requirements.explanation: Animation is a nice-to-have enhancement, not a core need.

requirements.text: The site needs to work on mobile devices
requirements.explanation: Over 60% of web traffic is mobile. This is non-negotiable.

requirements.text: Include a contact form so visitors can reach us
requirements.explanation: A contact form is strongly recommended for lead generation, but not strictly required.

requirements.text: Maybe add a blog section at some point
requirements.explanation: A blog is a nice future enhancement — low priority for launch.

teachingMoment: Sorting requirements by priority is the foundation of a good brief. 'Must' items are non-negotiable, 'Should' items add real value, and 'Could' items are wishlist items.
